section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
paragraphs.text: Prepared by: Cretu Giulio Cristian

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
1. Games Sold as Products, Treated as Temporary Services
paragraphs.text: Digital games are often governed by restrictive End-User License Agreements (EULAs), allowing publishers to:

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
1. Games Sold as Products, Treated as Temporary Services
paragraphs.text: Revoke access after purchase,

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
1. Games Sold as Products, Treated as Temporary Services
paragraphs.text: Shut down servers that make games inoperable,

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
1. Games Sold as Products, Treated as Temporary Services
paragraphs.text: Avoid any obligation to maintain functionality or offer refunds.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
1. Games Sold as Products, Treated as Temporary Services
paragraphs.text: This undermines the concept of ownership in the digital age. What consumers believe they are buying as a permanent product can disappear at the publisher's discretion.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
2. High-Profile Examples of Game Destruction
paragraphs.text: The Crew (Ubisoft, 2024): Entirely disabled—even in single-player—when servers were shut down. The game remained on sale until shortly before deactivation.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
2. High-Profile Examples of Game Destruction
paragraphs.text: Darkspore (EA): A single-player RPG rendered permanently unplayable due to always-online DRM.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
2. High-Profile Examples of Game Destruction
paragraphs.text: Battlefield Legacy Titles (EA): Battlefield 2142, Vietnam, and others became unplayable following server shutdowns. No official offline options were provided.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
2. High-Profile Examples of Game Destruction
paragraphs.text: Forza Horizon 1 & 2 (Microsoft): Delisted and made unplayable due to license expirations and decommissioned servers.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
2. High-Profile Examples of Game Destruction
paragraphs.text: These cases illustrate how players who paid full price for a game can lose access without warning or compensation.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
3. Growing Public Support
paragraphs.text: The European Citizens' Initiative "Stop Killing Games" has gathered over 1.4 million verified signatures, calling for the EU to protect access to purchased digital games.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
3. Growing Public Support
paragraphs.text: This reflects a growing frustration from consumers across Europe who have invested time, money, and emotion into games they no longer have the right to use.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
3. Growing Public Support
paragraphs.text: Many videos and petitions from influencers, preservationists, and legal activists have reached millions of viewers, highlighting just how widespread and urgent the issue has become.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
4. A Legislative Window: The Digital Fairness Act
paragraphs.text: There is currently a real opportunity to act. The Digital Fairness Act, a pending EU regulation, aims to protect consumers in the digital space by tackling:

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
4. A Legislative Window: The Digital Fairness Act
paragraphs.text: Exploitative in-app purchases,

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
4. A Legislative Window: The Digital Fairness Act
paragraphs.text: Dark patterns and manipulative interfaces,

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
4. A Legislative Window: The Digital Fairness Act
paragraphs.text: Addictive mechanics and unfair monetization.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
4. A Legislative Window: The Digital Fairness Act
paragraphs.text: However, game preservation and access rights remain unaddressed in the draft text.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
4. A Legislative Window: The Digital Fairness Act
paragraphs.text: Activists and citizens are now pushing for an amendment to include legal safeguards against publishers deleting or disabling games after they have been sold. This includes:

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
4. A Legislative Window: The Digital Fairness Act
paragraphs.text: Prohibiting companies from revoking purchased content,

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
4. A Legislative Window: The Digital Fairness Act
paragraphs.text: Requiring publishers to provide offline modes or local server options after shutdown,

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
4. A Legislative Window: The Digital Fairness Act
paragraphs.text: Allowing community-hosted servers or preservation efforts when support ends.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
5. Cultural and Historical Preservation
paragraphs.text: Video games are a major part of Europe’s digital cultural heritage. Many games that shaped generations have already been lost forever due to publisher decisions:

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
5. Cultural and Historical Preservation
paragraphs.text: Command & Conquer: Tiberium Alliances,

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
5. Cultural and Historical Preservation
paragraphs.text: Age of Empires Online,

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
5. Cultural and Historical Preservation
paragraphs.text: MAG on PlayStation 3,

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
5. Cultural and Historical Preservation
paragraphs.text: Battlefield: Bad Company 2.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
5. Cultural and Historical Preservation
paragraphs.text: Without legal protections, there is no obligation for companies to preserve these experiences or even provide access for archival, educational, or personal use.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
6. Industry Trends Show Urgent Need for Regulation
paragraphs.text: Publishers are moving further into aggressive “live service” models:

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
6. Industry Trends Show Urgent Need for Regulation
paragraphs.text: Always-online DRM is now common, even in single-player games.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
6. Industry Trends Show Urgent Need for Regulation
paragraphs.text: Support cycles are short—2 to 5 years before shutdown.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
6. Industry Trends Show Urgent Need for Regulation
paragraphs.text: Publishers like Ubisoft and Blizzard have openly stated that consumers should not expect to “own” games in the traditional sense anymore.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
6. Industry Trends Show Urgent Need for Regulation
paragraphs.text: Without legal safeguards, this business model ensures that all modern games could one day vanish, even from users who paid full price.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
Conclusion: Protect Consumers and Preserve Games
paragraphs.text: The Digital Fairness Act offers a timely opportunity to protect not just individual buyers, but Europe’s digital future. Games are art, culture, history, and community. When they are deleted after sale, the loss is not only financial—but cultural.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
Conclusion: Protect Consumers and Preserve Games
paragraphs.text: We urge EU policymakers to:

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
Conclusion: Protect Consumers and Preserve Games
paragraphs.text: Prevent publishers from disabling games after sale,

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
Conclusion: Protect Consumers and Preserve Games
paragraphs.text: Require offline functionality or self-hosting options,

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
Conclusion: Protect Consumers and Preserve Games
paragraphs.text: Ensure transparency and provide reasonable end-of-life plans,

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
Conclusion: Protect Consumers and Preserve Games
paragraphs.text: Respect the rights of players and the longevity of digital culture.

section_path: Research and Findings Supporting Consumer Protection in Digital Gaming
Conclusion: Protect Consumers and Preserve Games
paragraphs.text: This moment can define the future of digital ownership in Europe—and potentially serve as a model for the world.
